page.title: Marbella Tennis Open 2026: Yacht Charter Guide

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella Tennis Open 2026 with a private yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Watch matches, cruise the coast, and enjoy VIP sunsets from €749.
data.summary: A practical guide to combining the Marbella Tennis Open 2026 with a yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Includes fleet options, pricing, and tips for watching matches from the water.
key_facts: Marbella Tennis Open 2026 runs in July at the Puente Romano Tennis Club.
Puerto Banús is 5 km from the tournament venue — 10 minutes by taxi.
Charter a boat for match days: depart Banús, watch tennis, then cruise the coast.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749 for 2 hours, all inclusive.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) from €4,719 for 4 hours with a free jet ski.
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.
Best sunset anchor spots: Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, 20 minutes from Banús.
Book via WhatsApp for pricing on larger yachts like Ferretti 94 or Canados 86.

faq.q: What is the Marbella Tennis Open and when is it in 2026?
faq.a: The Marbella Tennis Open is an ATP Challenger Tour event held at the Puente Romano Tennis Club on the Golden Mile. The 2026 edition is scheduled for July. Exact dates are confirmed by the tournament organisers closer to the event.

faq.q: Can I watch the tennis from a yacht?
faq.a: You cannot anchor directly at the Puente Romano courts—the club is inland. However, you can charter a yacht from Puerto Banús, watch a morning match, then cruise to the coast for lunch and a swim. Many guests combine a few hours on court with a sunset boat trip.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a Marbella Tennis Open charter?
faq.a: For a small group, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal from €749 for 2 hours. For a VIP experience, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a free jet ski from €4,719 for 4 hours. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees.

faq.q: How far is Puerto Banús from the tennis club?
faq.a: Puerto Banús is approximately 5 km west of the Puente Romano Tennis Club, about a 10-minute taxi ride. You can easily combine a match with a boat trip from the marina.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht for a full day during the tournament?
faq.a: Yes. Tier A yachts are available from 2 to 8 hours (€749–€2,299). Tier B yachts have a 4-hour minimum. For larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What are the best nearby spots for swimming and sunset?
faq.a: Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are two sheltered coves 20 minutes east of Puerto Banús, perfect for a late-afternoon swim. For sunset, head west toward the Cabopino dunes or simply cruise along the Marbella coastline.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for the Marbella Tennis Open?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website or contact us on WhatsApp for real-time availability and pricing on larger yachts. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance during the tournament period.

data.body_html:
The Marbella Tennis Open 2026 brings top-level ATP Challenger action to the Puente Romano Tennis Club on the Golden Mile. For visitors who want more than baseline hospitality, a private yacht charter from Puerto Banús turns a day of tennis into a full Mediterranean experience. Here is how to plan it, boat by boat.

Why combine tennis with a yacht charter?

Puerto Banús is 5 km west of the Puente Romano club — a 10-minute taxi ride. You can watch a morning quarter-final, grab lunch at a beach club, then board your boat at Banús by 15:00 for an afternoon cruise. The tournament schedule typically finishes by early evening, leaving you time for a sunset swim at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, both 20 minutes east of the marina.

Fleet options for match days

We operate from Puerto Banús with a range of vessels. For small groups, the Boat Rental Marbella Tier A boats are the best value: the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 passengers) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 passengers) from €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for a full 8-hour day. All inclusive — skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT.

For a VIP experience, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 passengers) — available in grey and white — starts at €4,719 for a 4-hour charter and includes a free jet ski. Other large yachts such as the Ferretti 94 (29m), Canados 86 (26m), and Maiora 26m (all 12 passengers) are available on request via WhatsApp.

Typical itinerary: match + boat

10:00–13:00 — Watch tennis at Puente Romano. The club has good seating and on-site catering.
13:00–14:00 — Lunch at a Golden Mile restaurant or beach club (e.g. La Sala or Nobu).
14:30 — Arrive at Puerto Banús, meet your skipper at the charter desk.
15:00–18:00 — Cruise east to Cala Cortés for a swim, then anchor off Cabopino for sundowners.
18:30 — Back at Banús for dinner or evening matches.

What is included in every charter

  • Licensed skipper (English and Spanish speaking)
  • Fuel for the full itinerary
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava
  • Light snacks (chips, olives, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%) — no surprises

Pricing summary

BoatLengthCapacityPrice (2h)Price (8h)
Astondoa 4012.5m9 pax€749€2,299
Azimut 3912.5m11 pax€749€2,299
Mangusta 8024m12 pax€4,719 (4h min)—
Other yachts14–29m10–12 paxOn request via WhatsApp

When to book

July is peak season in Marbella. The Marbella boat charter market is busy during the tennis open, so book at least two weeks ahead. We recommend the Mangusta 80 for groups wanting a statement vessel — the free jet ski is a bonus for active guests between sets.

Frequently asked questions

What is the Marbella Tennis Open and when is it in 2026?

The Marbella Tennis Open is an ATP Challenger Tour event held at the Puente Romano Tennis Club on the Golden Mile. The 2026 edition is scheduled for July. Exact dates are confirmed by the tournament organisers closer to the event.

Can I watch the tennis from a yacht?

You cannot anchor directly at the Puente Romano courts—the club is inland. However, you can charter a yacht from Puerto Banús, watch a morning match, then cruise to the coast for lunch and a swim. Many guests combine a few hours on court with a sunset boat trip.

Which boats are best for a Marbella Tennis Open charter?

For a small group, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal from €749 for 2 hours. For a VIP experience, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a free jet ski from €4,719 for 4 hours. All depart from Puerto Banús.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees.

How far is Puerto Banús from the tennis club?

Puerto Banús is approximately 5 km west of the Puente Romano Tennis Club, about a 10-minute taxi ride. You can easily combine a match with a boat trip from the marina.

Can I charter a yacht for a full day during the tournament?

Yes. Tier A yachts are available from 2 to 8 hours (€749–€2,299). Tier B yachts have a 4-hour minimum. For larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp.

What are the best nearby spots for swimming and sunset?

Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are two sheltered coves 20 minutes east of Puerto Banús, perfect for a late-afternoon swim. For sunset, head west toward the Cabopino dunes or simply cruise along the Marbella coastline.

How do I book a yacht for the Marbella Tennis Open?

You can book directly through our website or contact us on WhatsApp for real-time availability and pricing on larger yachts. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance during the tournament period.
